result:
The cleaned data file has been successfully created at data/output/cleaned_data.csv with 16,111 records and 7 columns (NSID, nssec17, nssec18, nssec19, nssec20, nssec25, nssec32). All NS-SEC variables have been processed to:
1. Collapse fractional codes to major categories using floor()
2. Harmonize missing codes to standard scheme (-9=Refusal, -8=Insufficient info, -7=Prefer not to say, -3=Not asked, -2=Schedule not applicable, -1=Item not applicable)
3. Convert NA values to -3 (Not asked at fieldwork)
4. Retain only valid integer categories 1-17 for occupational classes
5. Convert to labelled factor variables with explicit labels for all 17 categories and missing codes

The file contains longitudinal NS-SEC data from ages 17, 18, 19, 20, 25, and 32, merged using full_join across all six waves to ensure complete NSID coverage.
